full_text:
Company/Organisation Information

2. - EHS Management Questions

Shipping and Receiving 
(relevant if the Third Party will be sending material back to GSK or to another 3rd party)

3. Environmental Protection

4. Chemical or Biological questions sets

5. Hazard Specific Risks

Animal allergens – relevant in the event that GSK is supplying lab animals – N/A

Radioactive Substances – N/A

Controlled Drugs – N/A

Process Safety

Please answer all questions as completely as possible and give a brief description where applicable or needed. In the event that a question or section does not apply, please mark as `NA’ (Not Applicable). | Please answer all questions as completely as possible and give a brief description where applicable or needed. In the event that a question or section does not apply, please mark as `NA’ (Not Applicable).
Company/ Organisation Name: | University of Helsinki
Site Address: | Biomedicum Helsinki, Haartmaninkatu 8, 00029, Finland
Total number of staff on site: | 
Completed by: | Dr. Anniina Färkkilä
Title: | MD, Ph.D
Tel. / Email Address: | anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi
Date Completed: | 11.1.2021

Risk Management and Compliance | Risk Management and Compliance
Please briefly outline the systems in place to ensure that all relevant EHS controls are identified and maintained to meet regulatory requirements | All the experiments are performed at the University of Helsinki research facilities in compliance with the University of Helsinki work safety regulations.
Is there any external verification of the EHS management system? | Work safety at the University of Helsinki is monitored by regular inspections by Valvira (National Supervisory Authority for Health and Safety).
Please describe the governance mechanisms to address any issues and drive continual improvement on performance. | 
People | People
Please identify training requirements and level of experience expected for personnel engaged in GSK activities. | All the work is performed by experienced researchers with Masters and Doctoral degrees with 10+ years of laboratory experience in natural sciences and / or medicine.
Fire Prevention & Protection | Fire Prevention & Protection
What level of fire detection and suppression control measures are in place for the areas what GSK will utilize. | All the laboratory facilities are equipped with smoke detectors-fire alarms, water sprinklers and centralized alarm system.
Performance | Performance
If there have been any significant adverse events in the last 5 years (e.g.

th smoke detectors-fire alarms, water sprinklers and centralized alarm system.
Performance | Performance
If there have been any significant adverse events in the last 5 years (e.g. fire, serious injury/illness or environmental damage) please provide a brief description. | NO
Occupational Health | Occupational Health
Outline the key aspects of the health program that covers the protection of work health when handling hazardous materials. | All the research personnel at the University of Helsinki are entitled to Occupational Health Care at the private health care center (Mehiläinen).

Shipping/Receiving | Shipping/Receiving
Do you have a specific program that covers the receipt, shipping, handling, use, dispatch and disposal of hazardous materials? | Yes (Fortum).

Environmental Protection | Environmental Protection
What processes are in place to ensure the site complies with all relevant environmental laws / regulation / permits/ approvals? | No hazardous substances are released to environment, all the wastes are sorted and collected and safely disposed by Fortum in compliance with the state laws and University of Helsinki internal regulations.
Outline the due diligence process for the disposal of waste streams generated as a result of GSK related activities. | 
Outline the processes to ensure effluent created from GSK processes will not compromise on or off site effluent treatment facilities. | No hazardous wastes are released into the sewage system.
Outline the processes to ensure all significant atmospheric emission points have been identified and adequately controlled e.g. filter, absorption, scrubbing etc.? | N/A
Outline processes to ensure ground contamination is minimized e.g. hard surface storage areas, bund or integrity testing of underground tanks | N/A

Chemical Hazards | Chemical Hazards
Is the laboratory accredited by a recognized accrediting body? | Yes.
Please outline the risk assessment process for work with chemicals. Please make reference to how these satisfy regional regulatory requirements. | Risk assessment is made on site for the chemical substance in question. Chemical substances are handled according to their MSDS. Toxic substances are handled in the fume hood by trained personnel wearing the protective gear. Chemical wastes are collected and discarded via Fortum. All the spills are collected and inactivated, contaminated surfaces are cleaned. Work safety at the University of Helsinki is monitored by regular inspections by Valvira (National Supervisory Authority for Health and Safety).
Please outline how risk assessments are communicated to lab personnel. | The new personnel receives a work safety training before starting laboratory work. Before using, a new chemical substance risk assessment is done in the laboratory according to MSDS.
Please outline the written standards / documents detailing the procedures for handling chemical / hazardous materials. | All the protocols include description of hazardous chemical and safe ways of handling them.
Please describe how personnel engaged in work with chemical agents are suitably trained and experienced/supervised to undertake the work in question.

nd safe ways of handling them.
Please describe how personnel engaged in work with chemical agents are suitably trained and experienced/supervised to undertake the work in question. | The new personnel is introduced to work safety training before starting laboratory work. Before using, a new chemical substance risk assessment is done in the laboratory according to MSDS.
Describe the engineering controls (e.g. fume cupboards) available for handling hazardous materials. | The research facilities are equipped with fume hoods, and the flow intensity is regularly inspected by technical staff.
Describe any other controls (administrative or PPE) to minimize exposure to hazardous materials. | Laboratory coats, protective gloves and safety goggles are always used when performing laboratory experiments.
Describe the occupational health arrangements in place to monitor staff that handle chemical agents. | University of Helsinki provides regular health checkups for its research personnel.
Additional Question - Materials with OEL < 1µg/m3 | Additional Question - Materials with OEL < 1µg/m3
Please describe the control strategy or provide a copy of your risk assessment for handling highly potent (OEL < 1µg/m3) materials.

dditional Question - Materials with OEL < 1µg/m3
Please describe the control strategy or provide a copy of your risk assessment for handling highly potent (OEL < 1µg/m3) materials. | Highly potent chemicals are handled with extra care, for example, by using double gloves, laboratory coats, safety goggles, by minimizing the contact time, and storing the wastes in the ventilated cabinets.
Please provide exposure data to support the ways of working identified above. |

Biological Hazard or N/A? | Biological Hazard or N/A?
Is the laboratory accredited by a recognized accrediting body? | Yes
Please outline the risk assessment process for work with biological agent. Please make reference to how these satisfy regional regulatory requirements. | Other than potential virus and / or bacterial infection risks originating from the patient samples this project does not involve any other biologically hazardous materials.
Please outline how risk assessments are communicated to lab personnel. | The new personnel is trained to work safely with biological substances before starting laboratory work.
Please outline the written standards / documents detailing the procedures for handling biohazards | All the protocols include description of biologically hazardous materials and safe ways of handling it.
Please describe how personnel engaged in work with bio-hazardous agents are suitably trained and experienced/supervised to undertake the work in question. | Personnel working with bio-hazardous material is represented by experienced researchers with masters and doctoral degrees with 10+ years of laboratory experience in natural sciences and / or medicine.
Have all personnel engaged in work with human bloodborne pathogens been offered the Hepatitis B Vaccine? | Yes
Please describe the engineering controls (e.g.

ces and / or medicine.
Have all personnel engaged in work with human bloodborne pathogens been offered the Hepatitis B Vaccine? | Yes
Please describe the engineering controls (e.g. biosafety cabinets) available for handling bio hazardous agents | The research facilities are equipped with fume and laminar hoods, and the flow intensity is regularly inspected by technical staff. Laminar hood surfaces are disinfected with UV irradiation twice daily, and regularly wiped with 70 % ethanol.
Describe any other controls (administrative or PPE) to minimize exposure to bio hazardous agents. | Bio-hazardous materials are handled with extra care, for example, by using long-sleeved thicker gloves, laboratory coats, and safety goggles. Bio-hazardous waste, surgical instruments and dishes in contact with the bio-hazardous material are treated using 2% Virkon solution in water according to manufacturer’s instructions followed by autoclave sterilization (instruments and lab ware). The disinfected solid wastes are burnt.
Describe the occupational health arrangements in place to monitor staff that handle biohazardous agents. | University of Helsinki provides regular health checkups for its research personnel.

Animal Allergens | Animal Allergens
Please comment on your entry/gowning and animal-handling procedures | N/A
Are staff aware of care policies in their work area or responsibility? | N/A
Please comment on your occupational health program and whether it includes zoonosis awareness (based on the species within the facility) and where necessary health surveillance. | N/A
What control strategies are in place to minimize allergen exposure and spread? (e.g. Facility design, Engineering, PPE etc)? | N/A

Radioactive Substances | Radioactive Substances
Please provide a copy of the license to handle radiation or explain the regulatory framework for the region. | N/A
Please advise who provides your Radiation Protection Advice. | N/A

Controlled Drugs | Controlled Drugs
Please provide a copy of the license to keep, dispense and use controlled drugs on site. | N/A
Please advise who provides your Controlled Drugs Advice | N/A

Process Safety | Process Safety
Please outline the basis of safety that has been applied to the design of chemical plant including assessment techniques. | N/A
Please identify the specialist advice available for process safety (e.g. competent engineer)? | N/A
Please describe the arrangements in place for controlling work on site by visiting contractors? | N/A
Please outline controls to minimise risk from storage and transfer of flammable materials and gases. | N/A
Please outline the process to ensure any electrical hazards are assessed and controlled for areas handling highly flammable liquids / hazardous gases or in areas where dust is generated? | N/A
Please outline the permit to work systems in place. | N/A
